name: Guillermo J. Montenegro
headline: Software developer with more than 15 years of experience, focused on applied AI
summary: My experience started long before my first job: at age 12 I was already programming in Basic and Pascal, then in C and Assembly. At 20 I developed an operating system kernel based on what I learned from Andrew S. Tanenbaum. Today I work in applied research with LLMs, agents, and AI tools, alongside mobile and web development to take solutions from prototype to production.

highlights: AI Researcher and Senior Software Engineer focused on applied artificial intelligence research.
Experimentation with LLMs, development of agents and tools, and design of AI-assisted workflows.
Development of prototypes and solutions exploring model integration, deterministic behavior, and system orchestration.
More than 10 years of experience in mobile development with Android, iOS, and cross-platform technologies.
Participation in the design and development of mobile and web applications in corporate and research environments.
Experience building mobile applications and games with a focus on scalable, product-oriented solutions.
Development of enterprise and mobile applications in C#, VB .NET, ASP .NET, Objective-C, Java, and JavaScript.
Development of games and mobile apps including Rescue me from Asylum, AfterLife Samurai, and DimBot Unit 12.
Use of XCode, Eclipse, Blender, Gimp, Unity Engine, and Zebra scripting for label printing.
Internal auditor and senior quality control professional.
Leadership of a junior auditors team.
Implementation and control of ISO standards in production processes.

projects: Applied AI research with LLMs and agents
Prototypes for orchestration and determinism in AI

education.title: Electrical Technician with a specialization in Industrial Electronics
